recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Quadrantids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: Annual meteor shower with peak rate of 40-120 meteors per hour. Short, sharp peak lasting only a few hours.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Lyrids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: Annual meteor shower with peak rate of 10-20 meteors per hour. Best viewing after midnight in dark skies.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Eta Aquarids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: Meteor shower from Halley's Comet debris. Best viewed from Southern Hemisphere with rates up to 30 meteors/hour.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Perseids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: One of the year's best meteor showers with up to 60 meteors per hour. Known for bright fireballs.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Orionids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: Meteor shower from Halley's Comet debris with rates up to 20 meteors per hour.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Leonids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: Fast and bright meteors, occasionally producing meteor storms. Average rates of 15 meteors per hour.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Geminids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: The best meteor shower of the year with up to 120 meteors per hour. Bright, colorful meteors.

recurringEvents.title: ☄️ Ursids Meteor Shower
recurringEvents.description: Minor meteor shower with rates around 10 meteors per hour. Coincides with the winter solstice period.

events.title: 🌑 Total Lunar Eclipse
events.description: Total lunar eclipse visible across North America. Maximum eclipse at 11:33 UTC. The Moon will turn deep red during totality.
events.visibility: North America, parts of South America

events.title: ☀️ Total Solar Eclipse
events.description: Total solar eclipse with path crossing Greenland, Iceland, and Spain. Duration of totality up to 2 minutes 18 seconds.
